headline: 10 core contracts catalogued on Ethereum mainnet; admin multisig composition unverified

findings.text: Official deployments doc found at https://docs.liquidcollective.io/eth/deployments.md (via search result) listing 8 core contracts on Ethereum mainnet with roles: River (LsETH token), OperatorsRegistry, Oracle, Allowlist, Withdraw, ELFeeRecipient, RedeemManager, CoverageFund.

GitHub deployment table at https://github.com/liquid-collective/liquid-collective-protocol lists 10 Ethereum mainnet contracts including TLC token (0xb5Fe6946836D687848B5aBd42dAbF531d5819632) and ProtocolMetrics (0xf19345EabC46ADF82e85CC2293A657A2dBa5c7d4).
Protocol mentions audits from Halborn (Jul 2022), Spearbit (Sep 2022–Oct 2023), Quantstamp (May 2024), and Certora (Nov 2024). Audit scope addresses not extracted—would require fetching PDF reports.
Admin structure identified: Liquid Collective uses an administrative multisig made up of ecosystem participants, but specific signers, threshold, and address not disclosed in public docs.
Etherscan name-tag search and direct doc fetch returned core protocol addresses; no additional contracts surfaced beyond the deployment table.

rationale.verdict: Discovery slice: catalogued 10 contracts across Ethereum mainnet with full role coverage (token, staking pool, registry, oracle, allowlist, withdrawal, fee recipient, redemptions, coverage fund, governance token, and metrics). Administrative multisig existence confirmed but composition (signers, threshold, address) requires on-chain inspection via proxy patterns or direct contract reads. No addresses discovered yet from audit PDFs or factory enumerations.

evidence.shows: Protocol homepage with links to docs, audits, and GitHub; confirms Liquid Collective is liquid staking protocol built by multiple teams including Coinbase, Figment, Staked, Blockdaemon, Galaxy, ParaFi, etc.

Public smart contract repository with deployment table listing River (0x8c1BEd5b9a0928467c9B1341Da1D7BD5e10b6549), OperatorsRegistry (0x1235f1b60df026B2620e48E735C422425E06b725), Oracle (0x895a57eD71025D51fe4080530A3489D92E230683), Allowlist (0xebc83Bb472b2816Ec5B5de8D34F0eFc9088BB2ce), Withdraw (0x0AFd81862eEA47322Cf85Db39D3D07e8A3c25154), ELFeeRecipient (0x7D16d2c4e96BCFC8f815E15b771aC847EcbDB48b), RedeemManager (0x080b3a41390b357Ad7e8097644d1DEDf57AD3375), CoverageFund (0x32aac358b627b9feaa971cc33304027a41e49a81), TLC (0xb5Fe6946836D687848B5aBd42dAbF531d5819632), ProtocolMetrics (0xf19345EabC46ADF82e85CC2293A657A2dBa5c7d4).
Security audits page listing four audit firms: Halborn (Jul 2022), Spearbit (Sep 2022, May 2023, Jul 2023, Oct 2023), Quantstamp (May 2024), Certora (Nov 2024).
ETH liquid staking documentation site confirming River as core staking contract and upgradeable proxy pattern for contract upgrades.
Etherscan token page for LsETH (River) confirming Ethereum mainnet deployment and ERC-20 token status.

Protocol FAQs confirming administrative multisig governance: 'A multi-signature wallet with ecosystem participants governs the protocol' and will transition to 'more programmable, onchain execution over time.' Withdrawal keys immutable (set to protocol contract), validator keys managed by node operators.

unknowns: D3-offchain: Audit PDFs not fetched; in-scope contract addresses from Halborn (2022), Spearbit (2022–2023), Quantstamp (2024), Certora (2024) reports unavailable without PDF fetch.
D1-offchain: Etherscan name-tag search blocked by direct fetch limitations; relying on GitHub and docs instead.
D6: No factory or child contract enumeration performed; River and OperatorsRegistry may have enumerable views but not checked in this run.

D7-offchain: Administrative multisig address, signer count, signer identities, and threshold not publicly disclosed in fetched docs. On-chain inspection required via proxy admin reading or direct contract calls.

protocol_metadata.about: Liquid Collective is a secure, institutional-grade liquid staking protocol enabling users to stake ETH and mint LsETH, a liquid staking token evidencing ownership of staked ETH and accrued network rewards. Built and governed by a multisig of ecosystem participants including Coinbase, Figment, Staked, Blockdaemon, and other industry leaders, the protocol emphasizes enterprise compliance with built-in KYC/AML and diversified node operators. It uses an upgradeable proxy pattern and includes a coverage program with Nexus Mutual to protect against slashing.
